what_it_does: Defines the GROUND role for a listening cognition cast: a Pydantic output schema (GroundOut) and a ROLE manifest that instructs a model to decide whether an operator utterance is in scope for the system and to return a one-line grounding note.
observations: Contains a single Pydantic model GroundOut with fields in_scope (bool) and note (str).
Defines a ROLE dictionary with metadata, prompt_template, output_schema, input_addresses, trigger, model_binding, mode_scope, rules, and render_hint.
The prompt_template asks the model to return ONLY JSON with in_scope and note.
Includes one rule 'ground-gates-inject' stating ground.in_scope co-determines recall injection.
Docstring notes this was migrated from cognition.py and is now the canonical definition.
standards: The docstring explicitly claims this is the canonical definition after migration from cognition.py.
in_scope is designed as a gate feeding recall injection.
The prompt combines a role identity, strict output format, and a JSON example.
conventions: Uses Pydantic BaseModel for structured output schemas.
Uses a top-level ROLE dictionary to declare role behavior and bindings.
Prompt template requests JSON-only output with an example object.
model_binding declares required capabilities ['chat', 'json'] and leaves default_model/default_base_url as None.
Uses Python set literal for mode_scope values.
concerns: The migration note references cognition.py but no migration details are present in this file.
default_model and default_base_url are None, so runtime binding is unspecified here.
The prompt says 'Return ONLY JSON' but does not show the exact schema or field descriptions inline.
No examples or tests for edge cases of in_scope are included.
notes: This file is a small, self-contained role manifest: one schema plus configuration telling the system how to fire a grounding listener and how its in_scope output gates recall.
questions: What exactly does live_state contain when this role fires?
What is the full text of the recall injection rule that consumes ground.in_scope?
How is default_model resolved when default_model is None?
What is 'Concurrent Cognition G2 · C2.3 listening cast' in concrete runtime terms?
How is the prompt_template rendered with the input addresses?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it defines a grounding role that checks scope and emits a citable note, and it exposes the metadata needed to fire that role.
apparent_themes: separation of schema, prompt, and runtime binding
intention_signals: It tries to make scope-checking a reusable, fireable listener role whose boolean output can be combined with recall relevance to decide whether to inject retrieved context.
novelty: The file treats 'in_scope' not just as an answer but as an explicit gate signal wired into a downstream recall injection rule, paired with a concise 'note' as a citable artifact.
contribution: Provides the structured output schema and role manifest for the GROUND listener, giving the system a deterministic way to ask whether an utterance is in scope and to receive a short grounding note for downstream gating.
summary_for_embedding: roles/ground.py declares the GROUND listener role and its Pydantic output schema GroundOut, which returns an in_scope boolean and a one-line grounding note used to gate recall injection.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to make utterance scope-checking a canonical, reusable, fireable role in the listening cognition cast.

symbols.does: Pydantic model that defines the GROUND role's output: a boolean in_scope and a one-line note.
symbols.description: GroundOut is a Pydantic BaseModel with two fields, in_scope and note. It represents the structured result of the GROUND role: a decision about whether the operator's utterance is within the system's scope and a short, citable grounding sentence explaining that decision.
